    <h1 {...{
      "id": "the-playground"
    }}>{`The Playground`}</h1>
    <p>{`The Playground is a feature of the Adobe UXP Developer Tool that allows you to experiment with the UXP APIs in a sandboxed environment.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`The Playground provides a low-barrier entry point for plugin prototyping, allowing you to experiment and iterate on ideas directly without scaffolding any full project on disk.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "how-to-use-the-playground"

        "href": "index.md#connected-applications"
      }}>{`connected`}</a>{` a supported application to UDT. In the top toolbar, click the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Playground`}</strong>{` tab to open the Playground panel. It will show you a code editor; click on the languages dropdown to switch between HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and Manifest editors.`}</p>

    <h3 {...{

    <p>{`Whenever you edit the code in any of the tabs (HTML, CSS, and JavaScript), `}<strong parentName="p">{`the Playground will automatically refresh the plugin`}</strong>{` in the host application to reflect the changes. If you want to get back to the initial state, you can click the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Reset`}</strong>{` 🔄 button.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert slots="text" variant="info" mdxType="InlineAlert" />

    <p>{`You can `}<strong parentName="p">{`troubleshoot the plugin`}</strong>{` by clicking the `}<strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`{}`}</inlineCode></strong>{` button to open the UDT debugger. This tool is based on the `}<a parentName="p" {...{

    <h3 {...{

    <p>{`The Playground is intended to be a prototyping tool to test APIs and iterate ideas quickly without having to scaffold a full project on disk. However, if you like how the plugin is shaping up, you can export the entire code (every file) into a folder that you can then load as a regular plugin.`}</p>
